steps.title: Contrast political strategies
steps.content: The text first describes Indigenous politicians in the United States winning congressional seats as members of the country's two established political parties. Then the phrase "on the other hand" introduces a different approach: Indigenous people in Canada and several Latin American countries have formed their own political parties to support candidates.

steps.title: Reject unsupported purposes
steps.content:
• Choice A - To trace the history of an Indigenous political movement and speculate about its future development: The text gives a brief contrast and one example from Ecuador, but it does not trace a full history or predict the future.
  • Choice B - To argue that Indigenous politicians in the United States should form their own political party: The text does not recommend one strategy over another; it presents both neutrally.
  • Choice D - To consider how Indigenous politicians in the United States have influenced Indigenous politicians in Canada and Latin America: No influence from the United States to other countries is mentioned.
The purpose is to highlight two approaches to Indigenous political representation. The answer is C.
